title: quercus lanuginosa var. brandisii
intro: Quercus lanuginosa var. brandisii, also known as Himalayan oak, is a variety of deciduous tree in the beech family, native to the Himalayas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Himalayan oak include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions, such as wounds, cuts, and eczema. Himalayan oak is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the form of powder or capsules in herbal medicine.
